name: cert_rotation
description: a TLS certificate rotation/expiry world; actions issue, rotate, expire, revoke

card.description: a TLS certificate rotation/expiry world; actions issue, rotate, expire, revoke

rules: A certificate is one of: none, valid, expired, revoked.

'issue' mints a fresh certificate only when none is live (status none, expired, or revoked): it bumps the serial, sets status to valid, and sets expires_at to clock + lifetime. Issuing while valid is a no-op.
'rotate' replaces a valid certificate with a new one before expiry: it bumps the serial, increments rotations, and resets expires_at to clock + lifetime. Rotating when not valid is a no-op.
'expire' advances the clock to the live certificate's expires_at and marks it expired; only a valid certificate can expire.
'revoke' immediately invalidates a valid certificate; only a valid certificate can be revoked.
issued counts every mint (issue and rotate); expired and revoked count lapses and revocations respectively.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action["name"]
    status = s["status"]

    if name == "issue":
        # Mint a fresh certificate only when no live one exists. Issuing while a
        # certificate is already valid is a no-op (use rotate to replace it).
        if status in ("none", "expired", "revoked"):
            s["serial"] = s["serial"] + 1
            s["issued"] = s["issued"] + 1
            s["status"] = "valid"
            s["expires_at"] = s["clock"] + s["lifetime"]

    elif name == "rotate":
        # Proactively replace the live certificate before expiry: new serial,
        # fresh validity window, clock unchanged. Only meaningful while valid.
        if status == "valid":
            s["serial"] = s["serial"] + 1
            s["issued"] = s["issued"] + 1
            s["rotations"] = s["rotations"] + 1
            s["expires_at"] = s["clock"] + s["lifetime"]

    elif name == "expire":
        # Time advances to the certificate's notAfter; a live cert lapses.
        if status == "valid":
            s["clock"] = max(s["clock"], s["expires_at"])
            s["status"] = "expired"
            s["expired"] = s["expired"] + 1

    elif name == "revoke":
        # Immediately invalidate the live certificate (e.g. key compromise).
        if status == "valid":
            s["status"] = "revoked"
            s["revoked"] = s["revoked"] + 1

    return s

emit.report: cert #{serial} {status}, expires day {expires_at} (now day {clock}); issued {issued}, rotated {rotations}, expired {expired}, revoked {revoked}
